records.name: __dbTotal
records.summary: Creates a new table containing summary records grouped by a key expression.
records.description: Creates a new table containing summary records grouped by a key expression. For each group, the specified numeric fields are accumulated and written to the output table.

parameters.name: cFile
parameters.description: Output table file name to create.

parameters.name: xKey
parameters.description: Grouping key. May be a code block, character expression, symbol, empty value to use the active index key, or NIL for no grouping key.

parameters.name: aFields
parameters.description: Array of field names whose values are summed in each group.

parameters.name: xFor
parameters.description: Optional FOR condition as a code block, character expression, symbol-compatible expression, or NIL for all records.

parameters.name: xWhile
parameters.description: Optional WHILE condition as a code block, character expression, symbol-compatible expression, or NIL for no WHILE restriction.

parameters.name: nNext
parameters.description: Optional maximum number of source records to process.

parameters.name: nRec
parameters.description: Optional single source record number to process.

parameters.name: lRest
parameters.description: When `.T.`, process from the current record rather than repositioning to the top.

parameters.name: cRDD
parameters.description: Optional RDD driver name used to create the output table.

parameters.name: nConnection
parameters.description: Optional connection handle used by RDDs that support connection-aware table creation.

parameters.name: cCodePage
parameters.description: Optional codepage identifier for the output table.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` when the summary table is created and populated successfully; `.F.` when no usable output structure can be created.

records.name: __DEFPATH
records.summary: Returns the current `SET DEFAULT` path with an appropriate trailing drive or path delimiter when needed.
records.description:
Returns the current `SET DEFAULT` path with an appropriate trailing drive or path delimiter when needed.

`__DefPath()` reads Harbour's current default path setting. If the path is not empty and does not already end with a path delimiter or drive delimiter, the function appends the proper delimiter. On drive-letter platforms, a one-character drive value receives the drive delimiter; otherwise the path delimiter is appended.

returns.name: cDefaultPath
returns.description: Current default path string with a trailing delimiter when required, or a null string when no default path is set.

records.description:
Deprecated compatibility function that fills one or more supplied arrays with field-structure information from the current work area.

`<aFieldNames>` receives field names. `<aTypes>` receives field type codes. `<aWidths>` receives field widths. `<aDecimals>` receives decimal counts. `<aFlags>` receives field flags when the Harbour build exposes that metadata.

The function returns the number of fields copied into the supplied arrays. If no current work area exists, or if no output array is supplied, it returns `0`.

Prefer `dbStruct()` for new code because it returns the field structure as a single multidimensional array.

parameters.name: aFieldNames
parameters.description: Array that receives field names from the current work area.

parameters.name: aTypes
parameters.description: Array that receives field type codes from the current work area.

parameters.name: aWidths
parameters.description: Array that receives field widths from the current work area.

parameters.name: aDecimals
parameters.description: Array that receives field decimal counts from the current work area.

parameters.name: aFlags
parameters.description: Optional Harbour-extension array that receives field flags when supported by the build and RDD.

returns.name: nFields
returns.description: Number of field entries copied into the supplied arrays. Returns `0` if no current work area exists or if no output array is supplied.

Prefer `Alias()` for new code.

use Customer new alias Customer

? Dbf()

records.name: DBFIELDINF
records.summary: Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD.
records.description:
Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD.

`<nInfoType>` selects which field information value to retrieve or set. Available values are defined in `dbinfo.ch`; some field-structure constants are also related to `dbstruct.ch`. `<nFieldPos>` is a one-based field position. `<xNewSetting>` is optional and is passed to the RDD as the proposed new value for info types that support changing a field setting.

A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error. If `<nInfoType>` is not numeric or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, Harbour raises a database-command argument error.

returns.name: xFieldInfo
returns.description: The value returned by the active RDD for the requested field information. The type and meaning depend on `<nInfoType>` and RDD support. If no current work area is open, `<nInfoType>` is invalid, or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, a runtime database-command error is raised.

records.name: DBFIELDINFO
records.summary: Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD.
records.description:
Returns or optionally changes information about a field in the current work area through the active RDD.

`<nInfoType>` selects which field information value to retrieve or set. Available values are defined in `dbinfo.ch`; some field-structure constants are also related to `dbstruct.ch`. `<nFieldPos>` is a one-based field position. `<xNewSetting>` is optional and is passed to the RDD as the proposed new value for info types that support changing a field setting.

A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error. If `<nInfoType>` is not numeric or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, Harbour raises a database-command argument error.

parameters.name: nInfoType
parameters.description: Numeric field information selector. Use constants from `dbinfo.ch`.

parameters.name: nFieldPos
parameters.description: One-based field position in the current work area.

parameters.name: xNewSetting
parameters.description: Optional proposed new value for info types that support changing a field setting. The value is passed to the active RDD.

returns.name: xFieldInfo
returns.description: The value returned by the active RDD for the requested field information. The type and meaning depend on `<nInfoType>` and RDD support. If no current work area is open, `<nInfoType>` is invalid, or `<nFieldPos>` is outside the current field range, a runtime database-command error is raised.

records.name: DBRLOCK
records.summary: Attempts to lock one or more records in the current work area and returns whether the lock request succeeded.
records.description:
Attempts to lock one or more records in the current work area and returns whether the lock request succeeded.

If `<xRecno>` is omitted or `NIL`, Harbour requests an exclusive lock for the current record. If `<xRecno>` is supplied, Harbour passes it to the RDD as the record identity for a multiple-record lock request.

A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error.

parameters.name: xRecno
parameters.description: Optional record identity to lock. If omitted or `NIL`, Harbour requests an exclusive lock for the current record. If supplied, Harbour requests a multiple-record lock using this value as the record identity.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: Returns `.T.` if the RDD lock request succeeds; otherwise returns `.F.`. If no current work area is open, a runtime database-command error is raised.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
use Customer new shared

if dbRLock()
   field->Name := "Updated name"
   dbRUnlock()
endif

Returns the work-area number for a relation defined in the current work area.

If `<nRelation>` is omitted, invalid, or `0`, Harbour uses relation number `1`. If the relation does not exist, or if no current work area is open, the function returns `0`.

endif

records.name: DBRUNLOCK
records.summary: Releases a record lock in the current work area.
records.description:
Releases a record lock in the current work area.

If `<xRecno>` is supplied, Harbour passes it to the RDD as the record identity to unlock. If `<xRecno>` is omitted, Harbour passes `NIL` to the RDD unlock operation, which releases locks according to the active RDD behavior.

A current work area must be open. If no work area is selected, Harbour raises a database-command runtime error.

parameters.name: xRecno
parameters.description: Optional record identity to unlock. If omitted, Harbour passes `NIL` to the RDD unlock operation.

returns.description: Does not return a value. If no current work area is open, a runtime database-command error is raised.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
use Customer new shared

if dbRLock()
   field->Name := "Updated name"
   dbRUnlock()
endif

records.name: DMY

records.description: Formats a date as day-month-name-year text using the active Harbour language and current `SET CENTURY` state. When `<lMode>` is `.T.`, a period is appended after a one-digit day. If the date argument is omitted or is not a date/timestamp, the current date is used.

parameters.name: dDate

parameters.name: lMode
parameters.description: When `.T.`, appends a period after one-digit day values.

returns.name: cDateString
returns.description: Formatted localized date string, or a null string if the month component is invalid.

records.name: EVAL
records.summary: Evaluates a codeblock and returns the value produced by that codeblock.
records.description:
Evaluates a codeblock and returns the value produced by that codeblock.

`<bBlock>` is the codeblock to evaluate. Optional arguments after `<bBlock>` are passed to the codeblock. Harbour pushes the supplied block and arguments and executes the block with the optional arguments. If the first argument is not a codeblock, Harbour raises a base runtime error.

parameters.name: xArg
parameters.description: First optional argument passed to the codeblock. Additional arguments may follow. Reference arguments are preserved by the implementation.

returns.name: xResult
returns.description: Value returned by evaluating `<bBlock>`. The value can be of any Harbour type.

returns.name: nResult
returns.description: Factorial result, `-1` for out-of-range numeric input, or CT error-substitution behavior for invalid arguments.

returns.name: nHandle
returns.description: Numeric file handle, or `F_ERROR` on failure.

nHandle := FCreate( "sample.txt" )
if nHandle != F_ERROR

endif

records.name: Hash
records.summary: Creates and returns a hash from alternating key/value arguments.
records.description:
Creates and returns a hash from alternating key/value arguments.

Arguments are supplied as key/value pairs: `<xKey1>, <xValue1>, <xKey2>, <xValue2>, ...`. The number of arguments must be even. Each key must be a valid Harbour hash key type.

If an odd number of arguments is supplied, or a supplied key is not a valid hash key, Harbour raises a runtime error.

returns.name: hHash
returns.description: New hash containing the supplied key/value pairs.

records.description: Tests the first byte of `<cText>` and returns whether it is an ASCII alphabetic letter. The test is ASCII-only and does not use the current Harbour codepage. Non-string or empty input returns `.F.`.

parameters.name: cText
parameters.description: String whose first byte is tested.

returns.name: lIsMatch
returns.description: `.T.` when the first byte is an ASCII alphabetic letter; otherwise `.F.`.

parameters.name: cText
parameters.description: String whose first byte is tested.

returns.name: lIsMatch
returns.description: `.T.` when the first byte is an ASCII decimal digit; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ASCIIISLOWER
records.summary: Tests the first byte of `<cText>` and returns whether it is an ASCII lowercase letter.
records.description: Tests the first byte of `<cText>` and returns whether it is an ASCII lowercase letter. The test is ASCII-only and does not use the current Harbour codepage. Non-string or empty input returns `.F.`.

parameters.description: String whose first byte is tested.

returns.name: lIsMatch
returns.description: `.T.` when the first byte is an ASCII lowercase letter; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ASCIILOWER
records.summary: Returns an ASCII-only lowercase copy of `<cText>`.
records.description: Returns an ASCII-only lowercase copy of `<cText>`. Only ASCII uppercase letters are changed; the current Harbour codepage is not used. Invalid argument types raise a runtime argument error.

parameters.name: cText
parameters.description: String to convert using ASCII-only lowercase rules.

returns.name: cLower
returns.description: Converted string. If no ASCII uppercase bytes are present, Harbour may return the original string item.

records.name: HB_ASCIIUPPER
records.summary: Returns an ASCII-only uppercase copy of `<cText>`.
records.description: Returns an ASCII-only uppercase copy of `<cText>`. Only ASCII lowercase letters are changed; the current Harbour codepage is not used. Invalid argument types raise a runtime argument error.

parameters.name: cText
parameters.description: String to convert using ASCII-only uppercase rules.

returns.name: cUpper
returns.description: Converted string. If no ASCII lowercase bytes are present, Harbour may return the original string item.

records.name: HB_AT
records.summary: Returns the one-based position of a substring in a codepage string, with optional range handling.
records.description: Returns the one-based position of a substring in a codepage string, with optional range handling.

parameters.name: nStart
parameters.description: One-based starting position. Values less than or equal to `1` start at the beginning.

parameters.name: nEnd
parameters.description: Optional ending position for the search range.

returns.name: nPosition
returns.description: One-based position of the first occurrence in the requested range, or `0` when not found.

records.name: HB_ATI
records.summary: Performs a case-insensitive search for a substring in a string using the active Harbour codepage and returns the one-based position of the first match.
records.description: Performs a case-insensitive search for a substring in a string using the active Harbour codepage and returns the one-based position of the first match.

parameters.name: nStart
parameters.description: One-based starting position for the search. Values less than or equal to 1 start at the beginning of the string.

parameters.name: nEnd
parameters.description: Optional one-based ending position for the search range. If omitted, the search continues to the end of the string.

returns.name: nPosition
returns.description: One-based position of the first case-insensitive match, or 0 when the substring is not found.

records.name: HB_ATOKENS
records.summary: Splits `<cString>` into an array of tokens using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as the other `hb_Token*()` functions.
records.description: Splits `<cString>` into an array of tokens using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as the other `hb_Token*()` functions.

parameters.name: cDelimiter
parameters.description: Delimiter string, logical `.T.` for end-of-line delimiters, or omitted for spaces.

parameters.name: lSkipStrings
parameters.description: When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected while scanning.

parameters.name: lDoubleQuoteOnly
parameters.description: When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only.

returns.name: aTokens
returns.description: Array of token strings. Raises a base argument runtime error if the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_ATX
records.summary: Returns the substring matched by a regular expression and stores its start and length by reference.
records.description: Returns the substring matched by a regular expression and stores its start and length by reference.

parameters.name: xRegex
parameters.description: Compiled regex handle or pattern string.

parameters.name: lCaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether matching is case-sensitive; default is `.T.`.

parameters.name: nStart
parameters.description: Receives the 1-based start position of the match.

returns.name: cMatch
returns.description: Matched substring, or an empty string when no match is found.

records.name: HB_BASE64DECODE
records.summary: Decodes BASE64 text and returns the decoded bytes as a string.
records.description:
Decodes BASE64 text and returns the decoded bytes as a string.

The decoder skips characters that are not valid BASE64 alphabet values and stops when the input is exhausted. If `<cBase64Encoded>` is empty or not supplied as a string, the function returns a null string. The source implementation notes that this decoder assumes an ASCII-based machine.

returns.name: cDecoded
returns.description: Decoded bytes as a Harbour string, or a null string when input length is zero.

records.name: HB_BASE64ENCODE
records.summary: Encodes string bytes into BASE64 text.
records.description:
Encodes string bytes into BASE64 text.

`<cData>` is encoded using the standard BASE64 alphabet. If `<nLineLength>` is greater than `2`, CRLF line breaks are inserted while encoding; values less than or equal to `2` disable line breaking. If `<cData>` is empty or not supplied as a string, the function returns a null string.

parameters.name: nLineLength
parameters.description: Optional line length for CRLF insertion. Values less than or equal to `2` disable line wrapping.

returns.name: cBase64Encoded
returns.description: BASE64-encoded string, or a null string when input length is zero.

records.description: Searches for `<cSubString>` in `<cString>` using byte-oriented string matching and returns a one-based byte position.

returns.name: nPosition
returns.description: One-based byte position of the first matching substring in range, or `0` when not found.

records.name: HB_BLOWFISHDECRYPT
records.summary: Decrypts `<cCipher>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using ECB mode.
records.description: Decrypts `<cCipher>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using ECB mode. By default ANSI X.923 padding is removed; when `<lRaw>` is `.T.`, the decrypted block data is returned as-is.

parameters.name: cCipher
parameters.description: Encrypted binary string. Its length must be a multiple of 8 bytes unless empty.

parameters.name: lRaw
parameters.description: When `.T.`, return raw decrypted block data without removing ANSI X.923 padding.

returns.name: cText
returns.description: Decrypted string. Empty input returns an empty string; invalid parameters or invalid block size return no explicit value.

records.name: HB_BLOWFISHDECRYPT_CFB
records.summary: Decrypts `<cCipher>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using CFB mode.
records.description: Decrypts `<cCipher>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using CFB mode. `<cInitSeed>` must match the seed used for encryption.

parameters.name: cInitSeed
parameters.description: Optional initialization seed used to derive the CFB vector.

returns.name: cText
returns.description: Decrypted string. Empty input returns an empty string; invalid parameters return no explicit value.

records.name: HB_BLOWFISHENCRYPT
records.summary: Encrypts `<cText>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using ECB mode.
records.description: Encrypts `<cText>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using ECB mode. By default ANSI X.923 padding is used; when `<lRaw>` is `.T.`, the input is padded to an 8-byte block boundary with zero bytes.

parameters.name: lRaw
parameters.description: When `.T.`, use raw zero-byte padding instead of ANSI X.923 padding.

returns.name: cCipher
returns.description: Encrypted binary string. Empty input returns an empty string; invalid parameters return no explicit value.

records.name: HB_BLOWFISHENCRYPT_CFB
records.summary: Encrypts `<cText>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using CFB mode.
records.description: Encrypts `<cText>` with a Blowfish key generated by `hb_blowfishKey()`, using CFB mode. `<cInitSeed>` optionally modifies the initialization vector.

parameters.name: cInitSeed
parameters.description: Optional initialization seed used to derive the CFB vector.

returns.name: cCipher
returns.description: Encrypted binary string. Empty input returns an empty string; invalid parameters return no explicit value.

records.name: HB_BLOWFISHKEY
records.summary: Builds a binary Blowfish key schedule from `<cPasswd>`.
records.description: Builds a binary Blowfish key schedule from `<cPasswd>`. The returned binary string is used as `<cBfKey>` by the other Blowfish functions.

parameters.name: cPasswd

returns.name: cBfKey

parameters.description: String to modify. Passing it by reference allows in-place update when possible.

records.name: hb_DirBuild
records.summary: Creates a directory hierarchy, creating missing parent directories as needed.
records.description:
Creates a directory hierarchy, creating missing parent directories as needed.

`<cDir>` is normalized before use. The function returns `.T.` when the requested directory already exists or the missing hierarchy was successfully created. It returns `.F.` when `<cDir>` is not a string, when an intermediate path component exists as a file, or when a directory creation step fails.

parameters.name: cDir
parameters.description: Directory hierarchy to create or verify.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the directory hierarchy exists or was created successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_DIRCREATE
records.summary: Creates a directory.
records.description: Creates a directory. Returns `0` on success, the filesystem error code on failure, and `F_ERROR` when the argument is not a string.

returns.name: nResult
returns.description: `0` on success; otherwise a filesystem error code or `F_ERROR`.

records.name: HB_DIRDELETE
records.summary: Removes a directory.
records.description: Removes a directory. Returns `0` on success, the filesystem error code on failure, and `F_ERROR` when the argument is not a string.

returns.name: nResult
returns.description: `0` on success; otherwise a filesystem error code or `F_ERROR`.

records.description: Returns an array of directory entries matching a directory specification and optional attribute mask.

records.name: HB_DIREXISTS
records.summary: Tests whether a directory exists with the supplied directory name.
records.description: Tests whether a directory exists with the supplied directory name.

returns.name: lExists
returns.description: `.T.` if the directory exists; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: hb_DirRemoveAll
records.summary: Recursively removes a directory and its contents.
records.description:
Recursively removes a directory and its contents.

If `<cDir>` is empty or does not exist as a directory, the function returns `.T.`. When the directory exists, read-only attributes are cleared where possible before removal. Subdirectories are removed recursively, except symbolic-link directories are treated as entries rather than traversed. The function returns `.F.` when an erase or directory-remove operation fails.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the directory was removed or did not exist; `.F.` if a file or directory removal step fails.

records.name: hb_DirScan
records.summary: Recursively scans a directory tree and returns matching directory entries.
records.description:
Recursively scans a directory tree and returns matching directory entries.

`<cPath>` defaults to a null string, `<cFileMask>` defaults to the platform all-files mask when not supplied as a string, and `<cAttr>` defaults to a null string. The returned array uses the same entry structure as `hb_vfDirectory()` / `Directory()` style directory arrays. File names returned from subdirectories are prefixed with the relative subdirectory path.

parameters.name: cPath
parameters.description: Starting directory path. Defaults to the current relative root when omitted or invalid.

parameters.name: cFileMask
parameters.description: File mask used to match file names. Defaults to the platform all-files mask when omitted or invalid.

parameters.name: cAttr
parameters.description: Optional directory attribute mask. Include `D` to return matching directories as entries.

returns.name: aDirectory
returns.description: Array of directory entry arrays. Entries from subdirectories have relative subdirectory prefixes in their file-name element.

records.name: hb_DirSepAdd
records.summary: Returns a directory path with a trailing platform path separator when one should be added.
records.description:
Returns a directory path with a trailing platform path separator when one should be added.

If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. A separator is added only when the string is non-empty, is not a drive-only specification, and does not already end with the platform path separator.

returns.name: cDir
returns.description: Directory path with a trailing path separator when applicable; otherwise the original string or a null string for invalid input.

records.name: hb_DirSepDel
records.summary: Returns a directory path with unnecessary trailing platform path separators removed.
records.description:
Returns a directory path with unnecessary trailing platform path separators removed.

If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. The function preserves root paths, drive roots, and UNC-root-like forms where removing the separator would change the meaning of the path.

returns.name: cDir
returns.description: Directory path with removable trailing path separators removed; otherwise the original string or a null string for invalid input.

records.name: hb_DirSepToOS
records.summary: Converts path separators in `<cFileName>` to the platform path separator.
records.description: Converts path separators in `<cFileName>` to the platform path separator. If `<cFileName>` is not a string, the function returns a null string.

parameters.name: cFileName
parameters.description: File or path string whose separators should be converted to the current platform separator.

returns.name: cFileName
returns.description: Path string using the platform path separator, or a null string when `<cFileName>` is not a string.

records.name: HB_DIRTEMP
records.summary: Returns the temporary-directory path selected by Harbour's file-system layer.
records.description: Returns the temporary-directory path selected by Harbour's file-system layer.

returns.name: cTempDir
returns.description: Result follows source behavior.

records.name: hb_DirUnbuild
records.summary: Removes a directory hierarchy upward from the supplied directory while directories can be deleted.
records.description:
Removes a directory hierarchy upward from the supplied directory while directories can be deleted.

If `<cDir>` is not a string, the function returns `.F.`. If `<cDir>` exists, the function removes that directory and then moves upward through parent directories until deletion fails, the path becomes empty, no path separator remains, or a drive root is reached. If the initial directory does not exist, the function returns `.T.`.

parameters.name: cDir
parameters.description: Directory path to remove upward from.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the directory hierarchy was removed as far as possible or the starting directory did not exist; `.F.` if input is invalid or a directory delete operation fails.

records.name: HB_DISABLEWAITLOCKS

returns.name: lPreviousDisabled
returns.description: Previous disabled-wait-locks state.

records.name: hb_FileDelete

records.description:
Deletes files matching `<cFileMask>` and returns whether at least one file was deleted.

`<cFileMask>` may include a path and wildcard mask. Directories are not deleted. Optional `<cAttr>` controls the directory attribute mask used for matching; `D` is removed from that mask and symbolic links are included. Read-only files are made writable before deletion when possible.

parameters.name: cFileMask
parameters.description: File name or file mask to delete. May include a path.

parameters.name: cAttr
parameters.description: Optional attribute mask used when finding matching files.

returns.name: lDeleted
returns.description: `.T.` if at least one matching file was deleted; otherwise `.F.`.

Returns whether a numeric file handle refers to a device.

parameters.description: File name or path string to split.

returns.description: Directory/path component extracted from `<cFileName>`, or a null string when no directory component is present.

returns.name: lExists
returns.description: `.T.` if a directory entry exists with the supplied name; otherwise `.F.`.

parameters.description: New extension component. Use a leading dot when a dotted extension is desired.

returns.name: cFileName
returns.description: File name rebuilt with the supplied extension component.

records.description: Sets a default extension on a file name string only when the parsed file name does not already have an extension. If an extension is already present, the original extension is preserved.

records.description:
Splits a file name string into path, base name, extension, and drive components and stores the requested components into by-reference parameters.

`hb_FNameSplit()` does not return a useful value. Component results are returned through optional by-reference parameters. The extension component includes the leading dot when present. The drive component is stored separately where the platform/path syntax supports it.

records.name: HB_GTALERT
records.summary: Displays a GT alert dialog using the active graphic terminal driver.
records.description: Displays a GT alert dialog using the active graphic terminal driver.

parameters.name: xMessage
parameters.description: Message value passed to the GT alert layer.

parameters.name: xOptions
parameters.description: Options value passed to the GT alert layer.

parameters.name: xColorNormal
parameters.description: Normal color. Character color string values are converted with `hb_gtColorToN()`; numeric values are used as numeric colors.

parameters.name: xColorHighlight
parameters.description: Highlight color. Character color string values are converted with `hb_gtColorToN()`; numeric values are used as numeric colors.

parameters.name: nDelay
parameters.description: Optional delay or timeout value passed to the GT alert layer.

returns.name: nSelection
returns.description: The numeric option selected by the user, as returned by the active GT alert implementation.

records.name: HB_GTCREATE
records.summary: Creates a new GT instance and returns a GT handle pointer.
records.description: Creates a new GT instance and returns a GT handle pointer.

parameters.name: nStdInHandle
parameters.description: Optional standard input file handle for the created GT.

parameters.name: nStdOutHandle
parameters.description: Optional standard output file handle for the created GT.

parameters.name: nStdErrHandle
parameters.description: Optional standard error file handle for the created GT.

returns.name: pGtHandle
returns.description: A GC-managed GT handle pointer when the GT is created successfully. If creation fails, no pointer is returned.

records.name: HB_GTINFO
records.summary: Gets or changes a graphic-terminal driver setting.
records.description: Gets or changes a graphic-terminal driver setting.

parameters.name: xParam2
parameters.description: Optional second value or operation parameter passed to the GT driver.

returns.name: xResult
returns.description: Result supplied by the active GT driver for the selected `GTI_*` operation. The value type depends on `<nInfo>` and the GT driver.

records.name: HB_GTLOCK
records.summary: Locks the current GT console to permit output only from the current process or thread.
records.description: Locks the current GT console to permit output only from the current process or thread.

records.description: Reloads the active GT using a named GT driver.

parameters.description: Optional standard input file handle for the reloaded GT.

parameters.name: nStdOutHandle
parameters.description: Optional standard output file handle for the reloaded GT.

parameters.name: nStdErrHandle
parameters.description: Optional standard error file handle for the reloaded GT.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the GT was reloaded successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_HALLOCATE
records.summary: Allocates internal capacity for a hash.
records.description: Allocates internal capacity for a hash.

parameters.name: hHash
parameters.description: Hash whose storage capacity is preallocated.

parameters.name: nSize
parameters.description: Requested preallocation size. Negative values are ignored.

returns.description: Does not return a useful value.

records.name: HB_HASH
records.summary: Creates and returns a hash from alternating key/value arguments.
records.description:
Creates and returns a hash from alternating key/value arguments.

Arguments are supplied as key/value pairs: `<xKey1>, <xValue1>, <xKey2>, <xValue2>, ...`. The number of arguments must be even. Each key must be a valid Harbour hash key type.

If an odd number of arguments is supplied, or a supplied key is not a valid hash key, Harbour raises a runtime error.

parameters.name: xKey
parameters.description: Hash key. Arguments are supplied in alternating key/value pairs. Keys must be valid Harbour hash key types.

parameters.name: xValue
parameters.description: Value associated with the preceding key.

returns.name: hHash
returns.description: New hash containing the supplied key/value pairs.

records.name: HB_HAUTOADD
records.summary: Gets or changes auto-add behavior for a hash.
records.description: Gets or changes auto-add behavior for a hash.

parameters.name: hHash
parameters.description: Hash whose auto-add flags are queried or changed.

parameters.name: xAutoAdd
parameters.description: Optional logical or numeric auto-add setting.

parameters.name: xDefault
parameters.description: Optional default value to store in the hash before applying the auto-add setting.

returns.name: nPreviousFlags
returns.description: Previous auto-add flag mask before any optional change is applied.

records.name: HB_HBINARY
records.summary: Gets or changes binary comparison behavior for a hash.
records.description: Gets or changes binary comparison behavior for a hash.

parameters.name: hHash
parameters.description: Hash whose binary-comparison flag is queried or changed.

returns.name: lPreviousBinary
returns.description: Previous binary-comparison setting before any optional change is applied.

records.name: HB_HCASEMATCH

records.description: Gets or changes case matching behavior for a hash.

parameters.name: hHash
parameters.description: Hash whose case-match flag is queried or changed.

parameters.name: lCaseMatch
parameters.description: Optional new case-match setting. `.T.` enables case-sensitive matching; `.F.` enables case-insensitive matching.

returns.name: lPreviousCaseMatch
returns.description: Previous case-match setting before any optional change is applied.

records.name: HB_HEXTONUM
records.summary: Converts a hexadecimal string to a numeric value.
records.description: Converts a hexadecimal string to a numeric value. Leading spaces are skipped. Hex digits may be uppercase or lowercase. If a non-hexadecimal character is encountered after the leading spaces, the result is reset to `0`. If the argument is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

returns.name: nNumber
returns.description: Numeric value represented by `<cHex>`, or `0` when an invalid hexadecimal character is encountered.

records.name: HB_HEXTOSTR
records.summary: Converts hexadecimal text to a byte string.
records.description: Converts hexadecimal text to a byte string. Non-hexadecimal characters are ignored. Hex digits are consumed in pairs; if fewer than two hex digits are available, the function returns a null string. If the argument is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cHex
parameters.description: Hexadecimal text to convert to bytes.

returns.name: cString
returns.description: Decoded byte string, or a null string when there are not enough hexadecimal digits to form at least one byte.

records.name: HB_HFILL
records.summary: Assigns the same value to every existing key in a hash and returns the hash.
records.description: Assigns the same value to every existing key in a hash and returns the hash.

parameters.name: xValue
parameters.description: Value copied into every value slot.

returns.name: hHash
returns.description: Result described by the function behavior.

records.summary: Computes the HMAC-SHA1 message authentication code for `<cMessage>` using `<cKey>`.
records.description: Computes the HMAC-SHA1 message authentication code for `<cMessage>` using `<cKey>`. By default, the return value is a hexadecimal string. If `<lRaw>` is `.T.`, the return value is the raw binary digest.

parameters.name: cKey
parameters.description: Secret key used for the HMAC calculation.

parameters.name: lRaw
parameters.description: When `.T.`, return the raw binary digest instead of hexadecimal text.

returns.name: cHMAC
returns.description: HMAC-SHA1 digest as hexadecimal text by default, or raw binary digest when `<lRaw>` is `.T.`.

records.summary: Enables, changes, or disables ZLIB compression for an `hb_inet*()` connection.
records.description: Enables, changes, or disables ZLIB compression for an `hb_inet*()` connection. Passing `HB_ZLIB_COMPRESSION_DISABLE` disables compression; otherwise the function opens a ZLIB stream with the selected level and strategy and attaches it to the connection. An optional key enables stream encryption support in the ZNET layer.

parameters.name: nLevel
parameters.description: ZLIB compression level, or `HB_ZLIB_COMPRESSION_DISABLE` to disable compression.

parameters.name: cKey
parameters.description: Optional encryption key for the ZNET stream.

returns.description: This function does not return a value. It raises a runtime error if the connection or stream setup fails.

records.name: hb_iniNew
records.summary: Creates and returns a new INI hash.
records.description: Creates and returns a new INI hash. When `<lAutoMain>` is omitted or `.T.`, the hash is initialized with an empty `"MAIN"` section. When `<lAutoMain>` is `.F.`, the returned hash is empty.

parameters.name: lAutoMain
parameters.description: When `.T.`, creates an empty `"MAIN"` section in the returned hash.

returns.name: hIni
returns.description: New INI hash, optionally initialized with a `"MAIN"` section.

records.name: hb_iniRead
records.summary: Reads an INI file into a hash by loading file text and passing it to `hb_iniReadStr()`.
records.description:
Reads an INI file into a hash by loading file text and passing it to `hb_iniReadStr()`.

`<cFileSpec>` may contain a platform path-list-separated list of possible INI files; the first existing readable file is loaded. If the file cannot be read, the parser receives a null string. The returned hash follows the same structure as `hb_iniReadStr()`.

parameters.name: cFileSpec
parameters.description: INI filename or path-list-separated list of possible INI filenames.

parameters.name: lKeyCaseSens
parameters.description: When `.T.`, keys and section names are case-sensitive. When `.F.`, parsed keys and section names are uppercased.

parameters.name: cSplitters
parameters.description: Regular-expression splitter used between keys and values.

parameters.name: lAutoMain
parameters.description: When `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section.

returns.name: hIni
returns.description: Hash containing parsed INI data. A `"MAIN"` section is included when `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.`.

records.name: hb_iniReadStr
records.summary: Parses INI-formatted text into a hash.
records.description:
Parses INI-formatted text into a hash.

`hb_iniReadStr()` supports full-line comments, inline comments, sections in `[Section]` form, key/value lines split by `<cSplitters>`, `include` lines, and line continuation when a trimmed line ends with ` ||`. When `<lKeyCaseSens>` is `.F.`, section names and keys are uppercased. When `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section.

parameters.name: lKeyCaseSens
parameters.description: When `.T.`, keys and section names are case-sensitive. When `.F.`, parsed keys and section names are uppercased.

parameters.name: cSplitters
parameters.description: Regular-expression splitter used between keys and values.

parameters.name: lAutoMain
parameters.description: When `.T.`, top-level keys are stored in a `"MAIN"` section.

returns.name: hIni
returns.description: Hash containing parsed INI data.

records.name: hb_iniSetComment
records.summary: Sets the line-comment and inline-comment markers used by Harbour INI parsing functions.
records.description: Sets the line-comment and inline-comment markers used by Harbour INI parsing functions.

parameters.name: cLineComment
parameters.description: Marker used to recognize full-line comments.

parameters.name: cHalfLineComment
parameters.description: Marker used to recognize comments that begin within a line.

records.name: hb_iniWrite
records.summary: Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.
records.description:
Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.

`hb_iniWrite()` first calls `hb_iniWriteStr()` to build the INI text. `<xFileName>` may be a filename string, in which case the function creates/overwrites the file and closes it, or a numeric file handle, in which case the handle is used but not closed. The function returns `.F.` if INI text cannot be generated, the file argument is invalid, the file cannot be created/opened, or the full text cannot be written.

parameters.name: xFileName
parameters.description: Filename string to create/overwrite, or an existing numeric file handle.

parameters.name: cCommentBegin
parameters.description: Optional comment text written before generated INI content.

parameters.name: cCommentEnd
parameters.description: Optional comment text written after generated INI content.

parameters.name: lAutoMain
parameters.description: Controls how the `"MAIN"` section or top-level entries are written.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the generated INI text is written successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: hb_iniWriteStr
records.summary: Converts an INI hash into INI-formatted text.
records.description:
Converts an INI hash into INI-formatted text.

`hb_iniWriteStr()` writes optional leading and trailing comment text, writes top-level key/value pairs or the `"MAIN"` section depending on `<lAutoMain>`, and then writes hash-valued sections in `[Section]` form. If `<lAutoMain>` is `.T.` but the hash has no `"MAIN"` section, it behaves as if `<lAutoMain>` were `.F.`. If `<hIni>` is not a hash, or if no output text is generated, it returns `NIL`.

parameters.name: hIni
parameters.description: INI data hash to convert to text.

parameters.name: cCommentBegin
parameters.description: Optional comment text written before generated INI content when non-empty.

parameters.name: cCommentEnd
parameters.description: Optional comment text written after generated INI content when non-empty.

parameters.name: lAutoMain
parameters.description: Controls how the `"MAIN"` section or top-level entries are written.

returns.name: cData
returns.description: Generated INI-formatted text. If `NIL` is returned, the input was not a hash or no output text was generated.

records.name: HB_ISARRAY
records.summary: Returns whether the argument is an array value.
records.description: Returns whether the argument is an array value.

returns.name: lIsArray
returns.description: `.T.` when the test succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_ISBLOCK

records.name: HB_JSONDECODE
records.summary: Decodes a JSON string and stores the decoded value into a by-reference variable.
records.description: Decodes a JSON string and stores the decoded value into a by-reference variable. Returns the number of processed characters/symbols.

returns.name: nLengthDecoded
returns.description: Number of processed input characters/symbols.

records.name: HB_JSONENCODE
records.summary: Encodes a Harbour value to JSON text.
records.description: Encodes a Harbour value to JSON text. Pretty-printing is controlled by a logical human-readable flag or numeric indentation setting; codeblocks encode as JSON null and objects encode as arrays per Harbour's JSON encoder behavior.

parameters.name: lHumanOrIndent
parameters.description: `.T.` for human-readable output, numeric indent value, `0` to disable indentation, or `-1` for tab indentation.

returns.name: cJSONString
returns.description: JSON encoded string.

records.name: HB_KEYCHAR
records.summary: Converts a Harbour key code to the corresponding character string when a character representation is available.
records.description: Converts a Harbour key code to the corresponding character string when a character representation is available.

returns.name: cChar
returns.description: Character string for the key code, or a null string when no character representation is available.

records.name: HB_KEYCLEAR
records.summary: Clears Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.description: Clears Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYCODE
records.summary: Converts the first character of a string to Harbour's extended key code representation.
records.description: Converts the first character of a string to Harbour's extended key code representation. Non-ASCII Unicode characters are encoded using Harbour's Unicode key representation.

parameters.name: cChar
parameters.description: String whose first character is converted to a key code.

returns.name: nExtKey
returns.description: Extended key code for the first character, or `0` when no character can be read.

records.name: HB_KEYEXT
records.summary: Converts a standard key code to Harbour's extended key-code representation.
records.description: Converts a standard key code to Harbour's extended key-code representation.

returns.name: nExtKey
returns.description: Extended Harbour key code.

records.name: HB_KEYINS
records.summary: Inserts a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values at the front of Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.description: Inserts a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values at the front of Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.

parameters.name: xKey
parameters.description: Numeric key code, string text, or an array containing numeric/string key values.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYLAST
records.summary: Returns the last key/event code recorded by Harbour's input layer, optionally constrained by an event mask.
records.description: Returns the last key/event code recorded by Harbour's input layer, optionally constrained by an event mask.

returns.name: nKey
returns.description: Last key/event code matching the requested mask.

records.name: HB_KEYMOD
records.summary: Returns the modifier-bit portion of a Harbour extended key code.
records.description: Returns the modifier-bit portion of a Harbour extended key code.

returns.name: nModifiers
returns.description: Modifier flags encoded in `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_KEYNEXT
records.summary: Returns the next key/event code from the keyboard buffer without removing it.
records.description: Returns the next key/event code from the keyboard buffer without removing it.

parameters.name: nEventMask
parameters.description: Optional event mask. When omitted, Harbour uses the current SET event mask.

returns.name: nKey
returns.description: Next key/event code, or `0` if none is available for the requested event mask.

records.name: HB_KEYPUT
records.summary: Appends a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values to Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.
records.description: Appends a numeric key code, a string, or an array of key values to Harbour's keyboard/typeahead buffer.

parameters.name: xKey
parameters.description: Numeric key code, string text, or an array containing numeric/string key values.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_KEYSETLAST
records.summary: Sets Harbour's last-key value and returns the previous last-key value.
records.description: Sets Harbour's last-key value and returns the previous last-key value.

returns.name: nPreviousKey
returns.description: Previous last-key value. If `<nKeyCode>` is not numeric, no explicit value is returned.

records.name: HB_KEYSTD
records.summary: Converts an extended Harbour key code to the standard Clipper-style key code.
records.description: Converts an extended Harbour key code to the standard Clipper-style key code.

returns.name: nClipKey
returns.description: Standard key code corresponding to `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_KEYVAL
records.summary: Returns the key-value portion of a Harbour extended key code.
records.description: Returns the key-value portion of a Harbour extended key code.

returns.name: nKeyValue
returns.description: Key value encoded in `<nExtKey>`.

records.name: HB_LANGERRMSG
records.summary: Returns the localized error message text for a Harbour error code using the active language table.
records.description: Returns the localized error message text for a Harbour error code using the active language table.

parameters.name: nErrorCode
parameters.description: Error code whose language-specific message is requested.

returns.name: cErrorMessage
returns.description: Localized error message text, or a null string when no matching message is available.

records.name: HB_LANGMESSAGE
records.summary: Returns a message item from the active or requested language table.
records.description: Returns a message item from the active or requested language table.

parameters.name: cLangID
parameters.description: Optional language id. When omitted, the active language is used.

returns.name: cMessage
returns.description: Requested language message text, or a null string when unavailable.

records.name: HB_LANGNAME
records.summary: Returns a descriptive name string for a language module.
records.description: Returns a descriptive name string for a language module. When a language id is supplied, that language is queried; otherwise the active language is used.

returns.name: cLangName
returns.description: Language description string, or a 'not installed' description when the language is unavailable.

records.name: HB_LANGNEW
records.summary: Creates/registers a translated language module by converting strings from one language/codepage pair to a new language/codepage pair.
records.description: Creates/registers a translated language module by converting strings from one language/codepage pair to a new language/codepage pair.

returns.name: lOK
returns.description: `.T.` when the translated language module is created/registered; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: hb_langSelect
records.summary: Selects a Harbour language module and optionally maps or applies a codepage.
records.description: Selects a Harbour language module and optionally maps or applies a codepage. Returns the previously selected language id.

parameters.name: cCodepage
parameters.description: Optional codepage id used when selecting or creating a translated language module.

returns.name: cOldLang
returns.description: Previously selected language id.

records.name: HB_LEFTEQ
records.summary: Returns whether the left side of the first string matches the second string using the current codepage comparison rules.
records.description:
Returns whether the left side of the first string matches the second string using the current codepage comparison rules.

The comparison is case-sensitive. Both arguments must be strings; otherwise Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String tested for the left-side match.

parameters.name: cPrefix
parameters.description: Prefix string to compare against the left side of `<cString>`.

returns.name: lEqual
returns.description: Returns `.T.` when `<cString>` begins with `<cPrefix>` using current codepage comparison rules; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_LEFTEQI
records.summary: Returns whether the left side of the first string matches the second string using case-insensitive current-codepage comparison rules.
records.description:
Returns whether the left side of the first string matches the second string using case-insensitive current-codepage comparison rules.

Both arguments must be strings; otherwise Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String tested for the left-side match.

parameters.name: cPrefix
parameters.description: Prefix string to compare against the left side of `<cString>` case-insensitively.

returns.name: lEqual
returns.description: Returns `.T.` when `<cString>` begins with `<cPrefix>` using case-insensitive current-codepage comparison rules; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_LIBERROR

records.name: hb_PathJoin
records.summary: Combines a base path with a relative path when the second argument is relative.
records.description:
Combines a base path with a relative path when the second argument is relative.

If `<cPathR>` is not a string, the function returns a null string. If `<cPathA>` is not a string or is empty, the function returns `<cPathR>`. If `<cPathR>` already has a drive specification or begins with a path delimiter, it is treated as absolute and returned unchanged. Otherwise, the directory part of `<cPathA>` is combined with `<cPathR>`.

parameters.name: cPathA
parameters.description: Base path used when `<cPathR>` is relative.

parameters.name: cPathR
parameters.description: Relative or absolute path to combine with `<cPathA>`.

returns.name: cPath
returns.description: Combined path when `<cPathR>` is relative; otherwise `<cPathR>` or a null string for invalid `<cPathR>`.

records.name: hb_PathNormalize
records.summary: Normalizes a path string by removing current-directory path elements and resolving ordinary parent-directory pairs.
records.description:
Normalizes a path string by removing current-directory path elements and resolving ordinary parent-directory pairs.

`hb_PathNormalize()` splits `<cPath>` on the platform path separator, removes `.` components, removes redundant empty components where applicable, and collapses a normal directory followed by `..`. If the resulting path is empty after normalization, it returns `.` followed by the platform path separator. If `<cPath>` is not a string, it returns a null string.

Source comments note that this normalization can be unsafe when symbolic links are present, because textual `..` simplification may not match filesystem traversal through symlinks.

returns.name: cNormalizedPath
returns.description: Normalized path string, or a null string when `<cPath>` is not a string.

records.name: hb_PathRelativize
records.summary: Attempts to express a target path relative to a base path.
records.description:
Attempts to express a target path relative to a base path.

`<cPathBase>` and `<cPathTarget>` must both be strings; otherwise the function returns a null string. The function first joins both paths to `hb_DirBase()`, splits them into path components, compares common leading components using file-pattern matching, and then returns a relative path when possible. If the paths are on different drive specifications, or if relative conversion is not possible, it returns the target path.

`<lForceRelative>` defaults to `.T.` and allows the function to produce `..`-based relative paths when the constructed relative location can be resolved from the base directory.

parameters.name: cPathBase
parameters.description: Base path used as the reference for the relative path.

parameters.name: cPathTarget
parameters.description: Target path to express relative to `<cPathBase>` when possible.

parameters.name: lForceRelative
parameters.description: When `.T.`, allows construction of a `..`-based relative path when it resolves from the base directory.

returns.name: cRelativePath
returns.description: Relative path when conversion is possible; otherwise the resolved target path, or a null string for invalid arguments.

records.name: HB_RANDINT
records.summary: Returns an ARC4-based random integer.
records.description: Returns an ARC4-based random integer. Range semantics match `hb_RandomInt()`: with no arguments, `0` or `1`; with one numeric argument `N`, `1..N`; with two numeric arguments, the inclusive range between the two endpoints.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Optional upper bound, or one endpoint when `<y>` is also supplied.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Optional second endpoint. If `<x>` is greater than `<y>`, Harbour swaps the bounds.

returns.name: nRandomInt
returns.description: ARC4-based random integer in the requested inclusive range.

records.name: HB_RANDNUM
records.summary: Returns an ARC4-based random floating-point number.
records.description: Returns an ARC4-based random floating-point number. Range semantics match `hb_Random()`: with no arguments, `0 <= n < 1`; with one numeric argument, `0 <= n < x`; with two numeric arguments, a value between the two endpoints.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Optional upper bound, or one endpoint when `<y>` is also supplied.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Optional second endpoint. If `<x>` is greater than `<y>`, Harbour swaps the bounds.

returns.name: nRandom
returns.description: ARC4-based random floating-point number in the requested range.

records.name: HB_RANDOM
records.summary: Returns a pseudo-random floating-point number.
records.description: Returns a pseudo-random floating-point number. With no arguments, the result is in the range `0 <= n < 1`. With one numeric argument, the result is in the range `0 <= n < x`. With two numeric arguments, the result is in the range between the two argument values, with the higher bound not included.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Optional upper bound, or one endpoint when `<y>` is also supplied.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Optional second endpoint. If `<x>` is greater than `<y>`, Harbour swaps the bounds.

returns.name: nRandom
returns.description: Pseudo-random floating-point number in the requested range.

records.name: HB_RANDOMINT

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Optional upper bound, or one endpoint when `<y>` is also supplied.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Optional second endpoint. If `<x>` is greater than `<y>`, Harbour swaps the bounds.

returns.name: nRandomInt
returns.description: Pseudo-random integer in the requested inclusive range.

records.name: HB_STOD
records.summary: Converts a `yyyymmdd` string to a Harbour date value.
records.description: Converts a `yyyymmdd` string to a Harbour date value. Strings shorter than eight date digits return an empty date.

returns.name: dDate
returns.description: Date value represented by `<cDate>`.

records.name: HB_STOT
records.summary: Converts a compact timestamp string to a Harbour timestamp value.
records.description: Converts a compact timestamp string to a Harbour timestamp value.

returns.name: tDateTime
returns.description: Parsed timestamp value.

records.name: HB_STRCDECODE
records.summary: Decodes text using C compiler string-literal rules.
records.description:
Decodes text using C compiler string-literal rules.

`<cString>` can contain quoted C-style string literal text and escape sequences. If `<lContinuation>` is passed by reference, the function supports continued or multiline decoding and stores `.T.` when decoding ends inside an unclosed quoted string.

The function returns the decoded string. If the syntax is incomplete or invalid for the requested mode, the function returns `NIL`. If `<cString>` is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String containing C-style quoted text or escape sequences to decode.

parameters.name: lContinuation
parameters.description: Optional continuation flag. Pass by reference to receive `.T.` when decoding remains inside an unclosed quoted string.

returns.name: cDecodedString
returns.description: Decoded string. If `NIL` is returned, decoding failed because the input syntax was invalid or incomplete for the requested mode.

records.name: HB_STRCLEAR
records.summary: Overwrites the bytes of a by-reference string variable with zero bytes for secure clearing.
records.description: Overwrites the bytes of a by-reference string variable with zero bytes for secure clearing.

returns.name: lResult
returns.description: Result follows source behavior.

records.name: HB_STRDECODESCAPE
records.summary: Decodes backslash escape sequences in a string.
records.description:
Decodes backslash escape sequences in a string.

`<cEscSeqStr>` is copied, C-style escape sequences are removed/decoded in the copy, and the decoded string is returned. If the source string is empty, the original empty string is returned. If `<cEscSeqStr>` is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cEscSeqStr
parameters.description: String containing backslash escape sequences to decode.

returns.name: cDecodedString
returns.description: Decoded string after processing escape sequences.

records.name: HB_STRFORMAT
records.summary: Formats a string using Harbour format markers and supplied values.
records.description: Formats a string using Harbour format markers and supplied values.

parameters.name: cFormat
parameters.description: Format string containing percent-style format specifiers.

parameters.name: xValue
parameters.description: First value used by the format string. Additional values may follow.

returns.name: cResult
returns.description: Formatted string.

records.name: HB_STRISUTF8
records.summary: Tests whether a string contains valid UTF-8 multibyte sequences.
records.description: Tests whether a string contains valid UTF-8 multibyte sequences. Pure ASCII text returns `.F.` because the source only sets the UTF-8 result after encountering a valid non-ASCII UTF-8 sequence.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String to test for UTF-8 multibyte validity.

returns.name: lIsUTF8
returns.description: `.T.` when the string contains valid non-ASCII UTF-8 sequence content and no invalid UTF-8 sequence is found; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_STRREPLACE
records.summary: Replaces byte-string fragments in a source string using a source/destination string pair, source/destination arrays, or a hash of replacement pairs.
records.description: Replaces byte-string fragments in a source string using a source/destination string pair, source/destination arrays, or a hash of replacement pairs.

parameters.name: xSource
parameters.description: Replacement source definition. It may be a string of one-byte source characters, an array of source strings, or a hash whose keys are source strings and values are replacement strings.

parameters.name: xDest
parameters.description: Replacement destination definition. It may be a string of one-byte replacement characters or an array of replacement strings. Omit this parameter when xSource is a hash.

returns.name: cResult
returns.description: Transformed string. If the source text or replacement source is empty, the original string is returned unchanged.

records.name: HB_STRSHRINK
records.summary: Shrinks or truncates repeated string content according to Harbour semantics.
records.description: Shrinks or truncates repeated string content according to Harbour semantics.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String to shrink from the right side.

parameters.name: nShrinkBy
parameters.description: Number of characters or bytes to remove from the right side, depending on current codepage indexing behavior.

returns.name: cResult
returns.description: String with the requested right-side portion removed, the original string when `<nShrinkBy>` is less than or equal to `0`, or a null string when the whole string is removed or input is invalid.

records.name: HB_STRTOEXP
records.summary: Converts a string value into a Harbour source expression that can recreate the same string when compiled or macro-compiled.
records.description: Converts a string value into a Harbour source expression that can recreate the same string when compiled or macro-compiled.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String to convert to a Harbour literal expression.

parameters.name: lForceEscape
parameters.description: When .T., forces an escaped e"..." string literal form even when a simpler quoted literal could be used.

returns.name: cExpression
returns.description: Harbour source expression representing the input string. If the first argument is not a string, NIL is returned.

records.name: HB_STRTOHEX
records.summary: Converts the bytes of a string to uppercase hexadecimal text.
records.description: Converts the bytes of a string to uppercase hexadecimal text. Optional `<cSeparator>` is inserted between encoded byte pairs. If the input string is empty, the function returns a null string. If either required string argument is invalid, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: String bytes to convert to hexadecimal text.

parameters.name: cSeparator
parameters.description: Optional separator inserted between encoded byte pairs.

returns.name: cHex
returns.description: Uppercase hexadecimal text representing the bytes of `<cString>`.

records.name: HB_STRTOTS
records.summary: Converts a timestamp string to a Harbour timestamp value.
records.description:
Converts a timestamp string to a Harbour timestamp value.

If `<lUTC>` is passed by reference, Harbour stores whether the parsed timestamp text represented a UTC-aware form. If `<cTimestamp>` is not a string, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: lUTC
parameters.description: Receives whether the parsed timestamp text represented a UTC-aware form.

returns.name: tTimestamp
returns.description: Timestamp decoded from `<cTimestamp>`.

records.name: HB_STRTOUTF8
records.summary: Converts a string from a Harbour/codepage encoding to UTF-8.
records.description: Converts a string from a Harbour/codepage encoding to UTF-8.

parameters.name: cCPID
parameters.description: Source codepage id. If omitted, the active VM codepage is used.

returns.name: cUTF8Str
returns.description: Converted/measured value according to source behavior.

records.name: HB_STRXOR
records.summary: Returns a string produced by XOR-ing each byte of `<cString>` with another string or numeric byte value.
records.description:
Returns a string produced by XOR-ing each byte of `<cString>` with another string or numeric byte value.

If `<xKey>` is a non-empty string, its bytes are repeated cyclically across `<cString>`. If `<xKey>` is numeric, its low byte is used as the XOR byte. If the key string is empty, or the numeric key is zero, the original string is returned unchanged.

Invalid arguments raise a base argument runtime error.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: Source string whose bytes are XOR-ed.

parameters.name: xKey
parameters.description: String key repeated cyclically, or numeric byte value used as the XOR key.

returns.name: cResult
returns.description: Resulting XOR-ed string. Returns the original string unchanged when the key is an empty string or numeric zero.

records.name: HB_THREADDETACH
records.summary: Detaches a Harbour thread.
records.description: Detaches a Harbour thread.

returns.name: lOk
returns.description: `.T.` when the detach operation succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_THREADID
records.summary: Returns the current Harbour thread identifier.
records.description: Returns the current Harbour thread identifier.

parameters.name: pThread
parameters.description: Thread handle item. When omitted, the current thread is used.

returns.name: nThreadId
returns.description: Numeric Harbour thread identifier.

records.name: HB_THREADISMAIN
records.summary: Tests whether a thread handle represents the main Harbour VM thread.
records.description: Tests whether a thread handle represents the main Harbour VM thread. When no thread handle is supplied, the current thread is tested.

parameters.name: pThread
parameters.description: Thread handle item. When omitted, the current thread is used.

returns.name: lIsMain

records.name: HB_THREADJOIN
records.summary: Joins a Harbour thread and retrieves its result.
records.description: Joins a Harbour thread and retrieves its result.

parameters.name: xRetCode
parameters.description: Receives the value returned by the joined thread startup routine.

returns.name: lOk
returns.description: `.T.` when the join operation succeeds; otherwise `.F.`.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
pThread := hb_threadStart( { || "done" } )
if hb_threadJoin( pThread, @xResult )
   ? xResult
endif

records.name: HB_THREADONCE
records.summary: Runs a one-time action guarded by a caller-supplied once-control item, and reports whether the current call performed the first-time action.
records.description: Runs a one-time action guarded by a caller-supplied once-control item, and reports whether the current call performed the first-time action.

parameters.name: xAction
parameters.description: Optional action to execute once, supplied as a code block or function reference.

returns.name: lFirstCall
returns.description: `.T.` when this call performed first-time initialization; otherwise `.F.`.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
static s_once

if hb_threadOnce( @s_once, { || ? "initialized" } )
   ? "first caller"
endif

records.name: HB_THREADONCEINIT
records.summary: Initializes a caller-supplied item once with a supplied value in a thread-safe way.
records.description: Initializes a caller-supplied item once with a supplied value in a thread-safe way.

parameters.name: xItem
parameters.description: Variable or item to initialize once.

parameters.name: xValue
parameters.description: Value assigned during the first successful initialization.

returns.name: lInitialized
returns.description: `.T.` when the item was initialized by this call; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: HB_THREADQUITREQUEST
records.summary: Requests that another Harbour thread quit.
records.description: Requests that another Harbour thread quit.

returns.name: lOk
returns.description: `.T.` when the request was accepted for delivery; otherwise `.F.`. A true result does not guarantee that the target thread will actually stop.

records.name: HB_THREADSELF
records.summary: Returns the current Harbour thread handle.
records.description: Returns the current Harbour thread handle.

returns.name: pThread
returns.description: Current thread handle item, or `NIL` when no current Harbour thread handle item is available.

records.name: HB_THREADSTART
records.summary: Starts a Harbour VM thread.
records.description: Starts a Harbour VM thread.

parameters.name: nThreadAttrs
parameters.description: Optional thread startup attribute flags, such as memvar inheritance/copy flags from `hbthread.ch`.

parameters.name: xStart
parameters.description: Startup routine, supplied as a function reference/symbol, code block, or function name string.

parameters.name: xParam
parameters.description: First optional parameter passed to the startup routine. Additional parameters may follow.

returns.name: pThread
returns.description: Thread handle item for the created thread. If creation fails, verify exact failure behavior with a runtime test before relying on it.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
function Worker( cText )
   ? cText
return NIL

pThread := hb_threadStart( @Worker(), "hello from thread" )
hb_threadJoin( pThread )

records.name: HB_THREADTERMINATEALL
records.summary: Requests termination of all Harbour VM threads managed by the runtime.
records.description: Requests termination of all Harbour VM threads managed by the runtime.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_THREADWAIT
records.summary: Waits for one or more Harbour thread handles to become signaled or finished.
records.description: Waits for one or more Harbour thread handles to become signaled or finished.

parameters.name: xThread
parameters.description: Thread handle item, or an array of thread handle items.

parameters.name: lAll
parameters.description: When waiting on an array, controls whether the call waits for all listed threads instead of one thread.

returns.name: nResult
returns.description: For an array wait, returns a thread index or count depending on wait mode, or `0` on timeout/failure. For a single thread wait, returns a numeric wait result. Verify exact edge-case return values with a runtime test.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
aThreads := { hb_threadStart( { || NIL } ), hb_threadStart( { || NIL } ) }
? hb_threadWait( aThreads, 5 )

records.name: HB_THREADWAITFORALL
records.summary: Waits for all active Harbour VM threads to finish.
records.description: Waits for all active Harbour VM threads to finish.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: HB_TOKENCOUNT
records.summary: Counts tokens in `<cString>` using a delimiter, optional quote handling, and optional double-quote-only handling.
records.description: Counts tokens in `<cString>` using a delimiter, optional quote handling, and optional double-quote-only handling. When `<cDelimiter>` is omitted or not supplied as a non-empty string, spaces are used as the delimiter. If the delimiter argument is logical `.T.`, end-of-line sequences are treated as delimiters.

parameters.name: cDelimiter
parameters.description: Delimiter string. If logical `.T.`, end-of-line sequences are delimiters. If omitted or empty, a space delimiter is used.

parameters.name: lSkipStrings
parameters.description: When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected so delimiters inside quotes do not split tokens.

parameters.name: lDoubleQuoteOnly
parameters.description: When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only.

returns.name: nTokens
returns.description: Number of tokens found. Returns `0` when the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_TOKENGET
records.summary: Returns the `<nToken>` token from `<cString>` using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as `hb_TokenCount()`.
records.description: Returns the `<nToken>` token from `<cString>` using the same delimiter and quote-handling rules as `hb_TokenCount()`.

parameters.name: cDelimiter
parameters.description: Delimiter string, logical `.T.` for end-of-line delimiters, or omitted for spaces.

parameters.name: lSkipStrings
parameters.description: When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected while scanning.

parameters.name: lDoubleQuoteOnly
parameters.description: When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only.

returns.name: cToken
returns.description: Requested token text. Returns an empty string when the token is not found or the first argument is not a string.

records.name: HB_TOKENPTR
records.summary: Returns the next token from `<cString>` starting at zero-based position `<nPos>`, and updates `<nPos>` by reference to the next scan position.
records.description: Returns the next token from `<cString>` starting at zero-based position `<nPos>`, and updates `<nPos>` by reference to the next scan position.

parameters.name: nPos
parameters.description: Zero-based starting byte position. Receives the next position for a subsequent call, or a position past the end when no further token is available.

parameters.name: cDelimiter
parameters.description: Delimiter string, logical `.T.` for end-of-line delimiters, or omitted for spaces.

parameters.name: lSkipStrings
parameters.description: When `.T.`, quoted substrings are respected while scanning.

parameters.name: lDoubleQuoteOnly
parameters.description: When quote handling is enabled, restricts quote recognition to double quotes only.

returns.name: cToken
returns.description: Next token text. Returns an empty string when no token is available or the first argument is not a string.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
nPos := 0
while ( cToken := hb_TokenPtr( "red,green,blue", @nPos, "," ) ) != ""
   ? cToken
enddo

`<cFileName>` is the file name to inspect. The function returns the link target text when available. If no target is available or the operation fails, the returned string is empty or `NIL` depending on the VFS/backend result. The function updates Harbour's file error state.

`<cTargetFileName>` is the target file name. `<cNewFileName>` is the symbolic link name. Returns `0` when the symbolic link is created successfully and `F_ERROR` on failure. The function updates Harbour's file error state.

parameters.name: cTargetFileName
parameters.description: Target file name to which the symbolic link points.

returns.name: nResult
returns.description: Returns `0` on success and `F_ERROR` on failure.

records.name: HB_VFLOCKTEST
records.summary: Tests a byte-range lock in a virtual file.
records.description:
Tests a byte-range lock in a virtual file.

`<nStart>` is the starting byte offset. `<nLen>` is the length of the range to test. `<nType>` optionally supplies lock flags; Harbour combines it with `FL_LOCK`. The function returns the process identifier reported by the backend for a conflicting lock, `0` when no lock blocks the range, or `-1` on error.

The function updates Harbour's file error state.

parameters.name: pHandle
parameters.description: Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API.

parameters.name: nStart
parameters.description: Starting byte offset of the range to test.

parameters.name: nLen
parameters.description: Length of the byte range to test.

parameters.name: nType
parameters.description: Optional lock type flags combined with `FL_LOCK`.

returns.name: nPID
returns.description: Process identifier reported for a conflicting lock, `0` when no lock blocks the range, or `-1` on error.

parameters.name: cBuffer
parameters.description: Writable string buffer that receives the bytes read.

parameters.name: nToRead
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read. If omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`.

parameters.name: nTimeout
parameters.description: Read timeout passed to the virtual file backend.

returns.name: nBytesRead
returns.description: Number of bytes read. Returns `FS_ERROR` on read error.

examples.title: Example 1

pFile := hb_vfOpen( "sample.txt" )

   cBuffer := Space( 100 )
