   hb_vfClose( pFile )
endif

records.name: HB_VFREADAT
records.summary: Reads bytes from a virtual file handle at a specific file offset into a string buffer passed by reference.
records.description:
Reads bytes from a virtual file handle at a specific file offset into a string buffer passed by reference.

`<cBuffer>` must be a writable string passed by reference. `<nToRead>` optionally limits the number of bytes to read; if omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`. `<nAtOffset>` optionally specifies the file offset; the default is `-1`, which is passed to the virtual file backend.

parameters.name: pHandle
parameters.description: Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API.

parameters.name: cBuffer
parameters.description: Writable string buffer that receives the bytes read.

parameters.name: nToRead
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read. If omitted, Harbour uses the current length of `<cBuffer>`.

returns.name: nBytesRead
returns.description: Number of bytes read. Returns `FS_ERROR` on read error.

records.name: HB_VFREADLEN
records.summary: Reads a requested byte count from a virtual file and returns the bytes read.
records.description: Reads a requested byte count from a virtual file and returns the bytes read.

parameters.name: pHandle
parameters.description: Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API.

parameters.name: nToRead
parameters.description: Maximum number of bytes to read.

parameters.name: nTimeout
parameters.description: Read timeout passed to the virtual file backend.

returns.name: cBuffer
returns.description: Bytes read from the file. Returns a null string when `<nToRead>` is less than or equal to `0`, or when a read error occurs.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
pFile := hb_vfOpen( "sample.txt" )
if pFile != NIL
   cData := hb_vfReadLen( pFile, 100 )
   hb_vfClose( pFile )
endif

parameters.description: Virtual file handle returned by `hb_vfOpen()` or another Harbour virtual file API.

records.name: HB_WEEK
records.summary: Returns the ISO 8601 week number for a date or timestamp value.
records.description:
Returns the ISO 8601 week number for a date or timestamp value.

If `<nYear>` is passed by reference, it receives the ISO week-year. If `<nDayOfWeek>` is passed by reference, it receives the ISO day-of-week number. If the first argument is not a date or timestamp value, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

returns.name: nWeek
returns.description: ISO 8601 week number.

records.summary: Matches a string against a wildcard pattern.
records.description: Matches a string against a wildcard pattern.

parameters.name: cValue
parameters.description: Text value to compare with the pattern.

parameters.name: lExact
parameters.description: When `.T.`, the whole value must match the pattern. When `.F.` or omitted, prefix-style wildcard matching is used.

returns.name: lMatch
returns.description: Returns `.T.` if `<cValue>` matches `<cPattern>` according to the selected wildcard mode; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_WILDMATCHI
records.summary: Case-insensitive wildcard string match.
records.description: Case-insensitive wildcard string match.

parameters.name: cValue
parameters.description: Text value to compare with the pattern.

returns.name: lMatch
returns.description: Returns `.T.` if `<cValue>` matches `<cPattern>` using case-insensitive exact wildcard matching; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: HB_WRITEINI
records.summary: Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.
records.description:
Writes an INI hash to a file or an existing numeric file handle.

`hb_iniWrite()` first calls `hb_iniWriteStr()` to build the INI text. `<xFileName>` may be a filename string, in which case the function creates/overwrites the file and closes it, or a numeric file handle, in which case the handle is used but not closed. The function returns `.F.` if INI text cannot be generated, the file argument is invalid, the file cannot be created/opened, or the full text cannot be written.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` if the generated INI text is written successfully; otherwise `.F.`.

parameters.name: param1
parameters.description:
if called with logical .T. parameter then return real screen width - 1.
It gives exactly the same result in all standard GT drivers so we are still Clipper compatible. The difference can appear in some extended GT drivers which have additional functionality, f.e. CTW GT which is upper level GT and add CTIII Window support. When it's activated then MaxRow() will return current window max row and MaxRow(.T.) real  screen (window 0) max row what is the exact behavior of MaxRow() in CT3.

CTW GT which is upper level GT and add CTIII Window support. When it's activated then MaxRow() will return current window max row and MaxRow(.T.) real  screen (window 0) max row what is the exact behavior of MaxRow() in CT3.

records.name: MEMOLINE
records.summary: Extracts one formatted line from a character string or memo text.
records.description:
Extracts one formatted line from a character string or memo text. Formatting uses a target line width, tab expansion, optional word wrapping, and optional custom end-of-line markers.

By default, `<nLineLength>` is `79`, `<nLineNumber>` is `1`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, `<lWrap>` is `.T.`, and `<lPad>` is `.T.`. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers. If no custom marker is supplied, Harbour uses the current SET EOL value or the console newline. `<lPad>` is an undocumented compatibility parameter and should be treated as legacy behavior.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: Source character string or memo text.

parameters.name: nLineLength
parameters.description: Formatted line width used when splitting the text into lines.

parameters.name: nLineNumber
parameters.description: One-based formatted line number to extract.

parameters.name: nTabSize
parameters.description: Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation.

parameters.name: lWrap
parameters.description: When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking.

parameters.name: xEOLs
parameters.description: Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings.
parameters.default: Current SET EOL or console newline

parameters.name: lPad
parameters.description: When `.T.`, pads the returned line to the requested line width. This is an undocumented compatibility parameter.

returns.name: cLine
returns.description: Formatted line text. With default padding, the returned string is padded to `<nLineLength>`.

records.name: MEMOREAD
records.summary: Loads the contents of `<cFileName>` into a string and strips a trailing EOF character (`Chr(26)`) when present.
records.description: Loads the contents of `<cFileName>` into a string and strips a trailing EOF character (`Chr(26)`) when present. Harbour extends the old Clipper read-size limit.

returns.name: cString
returns.description: File contents as a string, with a trailing EOF character removed when present, or a null string if the file cannot be loaded or the filename is invalid.

returns.name: nKBytes

parameters.name: cString

parameters.name: cReplaceHardCR

parameters.name: cReplaceSoftCR

returns.name: cNewString
returns.description: Translated string, or a null string when `<cString>` is not a string.

parameters.description: String content to write before the EOF marker.

returns.name: lSuccess

records.name: MLCOUNT
records.summary: Returns the number of formatted lines in a character string or memo text.
records.description:
Returns the number of formatted lines in a character string or memo text. The count is computed using the same line parser used by `MemoLine()`, including line width, tab expansion, wrapping behavior, and optional custom end-of-line markers.

By default, `<nLineLength>` is `79`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, and `<lWrap>` is `.T.`. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers. If no custom marker is supplied, Harbour uses the current SET EOL value or the console newline.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: Source character string or memo text.

parameters.name: nLineLength
parameters.description: Formatted line width used when counting lines.

parameters.name: nTabSize
parameters.description: Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation.

parameters.name: lWrap
parameters.description: When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking.

parameters.name: xEOLs
parameters.description: Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings.
parameters.default: Current SET EOL or console newline

returns.name: nLines
returns.description: Number of formatted lines found in `<cString>`. Returns `0` when the input cannot be processed as text by this function.

records.name: MLCTOPOS
records.summary: Converts a formatted line/column location in a character string or memo text into a string byte position.
records.description:
Converts a formatted line/column location in a character string or memo text into a string byte position. The conversion uses the same line parser used by `MemoLine()`, including line width, tab expansion, wrapping behavior, and optional custom end-of-line markers.

By default, `<nLineLength>` is `79`, `<nLine>` is `1`, `<nCol>` is `0`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, and `<lWrap>` is `.T.`. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: Source character string or memo text.

parameters.name: nLineLength
parameters.description: Formatted line width used when interpreting line and column coordinates.

parameters.name: nCol
parameters.description: Formatted column number within the selected line.

parameters.name: nTabSize
parameters.description: Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation.

parameters.name: lWrap
parameters.description: When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking.

parameters.name: xEOLs
parameters.description: Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings.
parameters.default: Current SET EOL or console newline

returns.name: nPosition
returns.description: Byte position in `<cString>` corresponding to the requested formatted line and column, using Harbour's legacy memo-line positioning rules.

records.description: Returns whether the left mouse button is currently down.

returns.name: lDown

records.name: MLPOS
records.summary: Returns the byte position where a formatted line starts in a character string or memo text.
records.description:
Returns the byte position where a formatted line starts in a character string or memo text. The line calculation uses line width, tab expansion, wrapping behavior, and optional custom end-of-line markers.

By default, `<nLineLength>` is `79`, `<nLine>` is `1`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, and `<lWrap>` is `.T.`. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers.

parameters.name: cString
parameters.description: Source character string or memo text.

parameters.name: nLineLength
parameters.description: Formatted line width used when locating the requested line.

parameters.name: nLine
parameters.description: One-based formatted line number whose start position is requested.

parameters.name: nTabSize
parameters.description: Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation.

parameters.name: lWrap
parameters.description: When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking.

parameters.name: xEOLs
parameters.description: Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings.
parameters.default: Current SET EOL or console newline

returns.name: nPosition
returns.description: Byte position where the requested formatted line starts, using Harbour's legacy memo-line positioning rules.

records.description: Returns the month component of a Harbour date/datetime value.

returns.name: nMonth
returns.description: Month number, `1..12`.

records.name: MPOSTOLC
records.summary: Converts a byte position in a character string or memo text into formatted line and column coordinates.
records.description:
Converts a byte position in a character string or memo text into formatted line and column coordinates. The conversion uses line width, tab expansion, wrapping behavior, and optional custom end-of-line markers.

By default, `<nWidth>` is `79`, `<nPos>` is `1`, `<nTabSize>` is `4`, and `<lWrap>` follows the legacy memo-line parser default. `<xEOLs>` is a Harbour extension: it may be a string end-of-line marker or an array of string markers.

parameters.name: cText
parameters.description: Source character string or memo text.

parameters.name: nWidth
parameters.description: Formatted line width used when translating the position.

parameters.name: nPos
parameters.description: One-based byte position to translate into formatted line and column coordinates.

parameters.name: nTabSize
parameters.description: Tab expansion width. Values outside the useful range are normalized by the implementation.

parameters.name: lWrap
parameters.description: When `.T.`, wraps at word boundaries where possible; when `.F.`, uses fixed-width line breaking.

parameters.name: xEOLs
parameters.description: Optional Harbour extension specifying custom end-of-line marker text or an array of marker strings.
parameters.default: Current SET EOL or console newline

returns.name: aLineColumn
returns.description: Array containing the formatted line and column coordinates corresponding to `<nPos>`.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
cText := "alpha" + hb_eol() + "beta"

aLC := MPosToLC( cText, 79, 8 )
? aLC[ 1 ], aLC[ 2 ]

records.name: ORDCONDSET
records.summary: Sets the pending order-condition information used by the next order creation operation in the current work area.
records.description: Sets the pending order-condition information used by the next order creation operation in the current work area. The condition can include FOR/WHILE expressions, scope controls, evaluation/progress behavior, descending/custom/temporary flags, and RDD-specific options. Returns `.T.` when the current RDD accepts the condition information; returns `.F.` when there is no current work area or the RDD rejects the condition.

parameters.name: cForCondition
parameters.description: Optional textual FOR condition used to limit records included when the order is built.

parameters.name: bForCondition
parameters.description: Optional FOR condition code block used to limit records included when the order is built.

parameters.name: lAllRecords
parameters.description: Controls whether all records are eligible before other conditions or scope options are applied. Defaults to `.T.`.

parameters.name: bWhileCondition
parameters.description: Optional WHILE condition code block. When supplied, the source also treats the order condition as REST-scoped.

parameters.name: bEval
parameters.description: Optional evaluation/progress code block passed to the RDD while building the order.

parameters.name: nInterval
parameters.description: Evaluation interval or step count associated with `<bEval>`.

parameters.name: xStartRecord
parameters.description: Optional starting record identity or RDD-specific scope item. The source accepts any non-`NIL` item.

parameters.name: nNext
parameters.description: Optional maximum number of records to process.

parameters.name: xRecord
parameters.description: Optional record identity or RDD-specific scope item. The source accepts any non-`NIL` item.

parameters.name: lRest
parameters.description: When `.T.`, limits processing to the REST scope from the current record.

parameters.name: lCompound
parameters.description: Legacy compatibility compound flag. The source notes that this argument is always `NIL` in Clipper 5.3 and is the compound flag in Clipper 5.2.

parameters.name: lAdditive
parameters.description: When `.T.`, requests additive order behavior so existing orders remain open where the RDD supports it.

parameters.name: lCurrent
parameters.description: When `.T.`, requests use of the current record or current scope while building the order.

parameters.name: lCustom
parameters.description: When `.T.`, requests custom-order behavior for RDDs that support manually maintained orders.

parameters.name: lNoOptimize
parameters.description: When `.T.`, disables RDD optimization of the condition.

parameters.name: cWhileCondition
parameters.description: Optional textual WHILE condition used to limit records included when the order is built.

parameters.name: lTemporary
parameters.description: When `.T.`, requests temporary or memory-backed order handling. The source leaves the actual storage decision to the RDD.

parameters.name: lUseFilter
parameters.description: When `.T.`, requests that the RDD respect active `SET FILTER` and `SET DELETED` state while building the order.

parameters.name: lExclusive
parameters.description: Harbour extension flag requesting an order/index that is not shared with other clients.

returns.name: lSuccess
returns.description: `.T.` when the pending order condition is accepted by the current RDD; otherwise `.F.`.

examples.title: Example 1
examples.code:
USE customer NEW
ordCondSet( "Active", { || field->Active } )
ordCreate( "customer.cdx", "ACTIVE", "Name", { || field->Name } )

records.name: ORDCREATE
records.summary: Creates an order/tag for the current work area in the specified order bag.
records.description: Creates an order/tag for the current work area in the specified order bag. `<cOrderBagName>` identifies the order bag or index file, `<cOrderName>` optionally names the order, and `<cKeyExpr>`/`<bKeyExpr>` define the key expression. If `<lUnique>` is omitted, Harbour uses the current `SET UNIQUE` setting. Optional constraint parameters are used only when the constraint name, target name, and relation-key array are all supplied.

parameters.name: cOrderBagName
parameters.description: Order bag or index file name in which the order is created.

parameters.name: cOrderName
parameters.description: Optional order/tag name within the order bag.

parameters.name: cKeyExpr
parameters.description: String form of the order key expression.

parameters.name: bKeyExpr
parameters.description: Optional executable code block for the key expression.

parameters.name: lUnique
parameters.description: When supplied, controls whether the order is unique. When omitted, the current `SET UNIQUE` value is used.

parameters.name: cConstrName
parameters.description: Optional relational-integrity constraint name. Used only when the target name and relation-key array are also supplied.

parameters.name: cTargetName
parameters.description: Optional target relation/table name for relational-integrity metadata.

parameters.name: aRelationKey
parameters.description: Optional relation-key array used with the constraint and target names.

parameters.name: lEnabled
parameters.description: Enables the constraint when complete constraint metadata is supplied.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL` when execution completes normally. Invalid parameters or a missing current work area raise a runtime error.

records.name: ORDKEYADD
records.summary: ordKeyAdd() adds a key to a custom or manually maintained order in the current work area through the active RDD.
records.description: ordKeyAdd() adds a key to a custom or manually maintained order in the current work area through the active RDD. The order can be selected by name or numeric position, optionally qualified by an order bag/index file, and the key value is passed through to the RDD. This API is meaningful only for RDD/order types that support custom key maintenance.

parameters.name: xOrder
parameters.description: Order selector, supplied as an order name or numeric order position. Omit or pass `NIL` to use the active controlling order where the RDD supports it.

parameters.name: cOrderBagName
parameters.description: Optional order bag or index file name used to qualify the order selector.

parameters.name: xKeyValue
parameters.description: Key value passed to the RDD for insertion into the selected custom order.

returns.name: xResult
returns.description: Result value returned by the active RDD for the custom-key add operation. Treat exact type and success/failure conventions as RDD-specific unless verified for the selected driver.

When `<lCurrentRec>` is `.T.`, the search continues from the current record instead of repositioning first.

returns.description: `.T.` when a matching key is found; otherwise `.F.`. Invalid pattern type or no current work area raises a runtime error.

records.description: Gets or sets RDD-level user data associated with a USRRDD driver.

returns.name: xData
returns.description: Current or previous RDD-level user data.

records.summary: Converts a Harbour value to expression-style text.
records.description: Converts a Harbour value to expression-style text.

returns.description: Result described by the function behavior.

records.name: VALTYPE

records.description: Returns Harbour's one-character type code for a value.

records.summary: Returns or sets the current GET/MENU variable name used by READ-related code.
records.description: Returns or sets the current GET/MENU variable name used by READ-related code.

records.description: Returns Harbour's version string.

records.name: W2BIN
records.summary: Converts an unsigned 16-bit numeric integer value into a 2-byte little-endian ByteString.
records.description: Converts an unsigned 16-bit numeric integer value into a 2-byte little-endian ByteString.

returns.name: cBinary
returns.description: Result described by the function behavior.

records.name: WAITFORTHREADS
records.summary: Waits for all active Harbour VM threads to finish.
records.description: Waits for all active Harbour VM threads to finish.

returns.description: Always returns `NIL`.

records.name: WAITPERIOD
records.summary: Tests whether a previously started or requested wait period has not elapsed yet.
records.description: Tests whether a previously started or requested wait period has not elapsed yet. This CT helper is used for elapsed-time checks.

returns.name: lNotElapsed
returns.description: `.T.` while the requested wait period has not elapsed; otherwise `.F.`.

records.name: WCLOSE
records.summary: Closes a CT window.
records.description: Closes a CT window. If no window id is supplied, Harbour closes the current CT window. The return value is the next/current CT window id reported by the window subsystem.

parameters.name: nWindowID
parameters.description: Optional window id to close. If omitted, the current window is closed.

returns.name: nNextWindowID
returns.description: Next/current window id after closing, or an error value from the CT window subsystem.

records.name: WEEK
records.summary: Returns the week number for a date using CT3 compatibility rules.
records.description: Returns the week number for a date using CT3 compatibility rules.

parameters.name: lSimpleWeekNumber
parameters.description: If `.T.`, uses simple day-of-year divided by seven logic.

returns.name: nWeek
returns.description: Result described by the function behavior.

records.name: WILDMATCH
records.summary: Matches a string against a wildcard pattern.
records.description: Matches a string against a wildcard pattern.

returns.name: lMatch
returns.description: Returns `.T.` if `<cValue>` matches `<cPattern>` according to the selected wildcard mode; otherwise returns `.F.`.

records.name: WOM
records.summary: Returns the week-of-month number for a date.
records.description: Returns the week-of-month number for a date.

returns.name: nWeekOfMonth
returns.description: Result described by the function behavior.

records.name: WOPEN
records.summary: Creates a CT window and returns its numeric window id.
records.description: Creates a CT window and returns its numeric window id. The first four coordinates define the window rectangle. The clear flag controls whether the window content is cleared; the color and visible arguments are Harbour extensions.

parameters.name: lClear
parameters.description: Whether to clear the window area.

parameters.name: iColor
parameters.description: Window color attribute or color string accepted by the source helper.

parameters.name: lVisible
parameters.description: Whether the new window is visible.

returns.name: nWindowID
returns.description: Numeric id of the created window, or a negative/error value from the CT window subsystem.

records.name: WORD
records.summary: Returns a numeric value coerced through Harbour's integer return path.
records.description: Returns a numeric value coerced through Harbour's integer return path. Harbour allows `Word()` to be used anywhere, unlike the Clipper note that limited it to CALL parameter lists.

parameters.name: nFloating
parameters.description: Numeric value to return through the integer return path.

returns.name: nInteger
returns.description: Integer numeric value corresponding to `<nFloating>`. If the argument is not numeric, Harbour raises a base argument runtime error.

records.name: WOY
records.summary: Returns the week-of-year number for a date, optionally using ISO-style week numbering.
records.description: Returns the week-of-year number for a date, optionally using ISO-style week numbering.

parameters.name: dDate
parameters.description: Date for which to compute the week number.

parameters.name: lIso
parameters.description: When true, use ISO week-numbering behavior where supported by the implementation.

returns.name: nWeekOfYear
returns.description: Week number for the supplied date.

records.name: WSELECT
records.summary: Selects a CT window and optionally brings it to the top.
records.description: Selects a CT window and optionally brings it to the top. If no window id is supplied, returns the current CT window id.

parameters.name: nWindowID
parameters.description: Window id to select. If omitted, no selection change is made.

parameters.name: lBringToTop
parameters.description: Whether to bring the selected window to the top.

returns.name: nCurrentWindowID
returns.description: Current or previously selected window id reported by the CT window subsystem.

records.name: WSETSHADOW
records.summary: Gets and optionally sets the CT window shadow color/attribute.
records.description: Gets and optionally sets the CT window shadow color/attribute. The source returns the previous shadow attribute.

parameters.name: xNewColor
parameters.description: Optional new shadow color, as a numeric attribute or color string.

returns.name: nOldColor
returns.description: Previous shadow color/attribute.

records.name: XTOC
records.summary: Converts a value to a compact binary/string representation.
records.description: Converts a value to a compact binary/string representation. Dates become `yyyymmdd` text, timestamps become compact timestamp text, numerics become an 8-byte little-endian double, logical values become `T` or `F`, and other values are returned unchanged.

returns.name: cValue
returns.description: Converted character/binary representation, or the original value for non-special input types.

records.name: YEAR
records.summary: Returns the year component of a Harbour date/datetime value.
records.description: Returns the year component of a Harbour date/datetime value.

returns.name: nYear
returns.description: Year component.
